clusters.label: Deeds carry natural consequences
clusters.desc: Wrongdoing inevitably produces suffering while righteousness brings reward.

clusters.label: Constant practice brings stability
clusters.desc: Persistent dedication to spiritual and moral practice creates unshakeable inner peace.

clusters.label: Followers show varied ambitions
clusters.desc: Different people have distinct goals and varying commitment levels toward their leaders and teachings.

clusters.label: Power flows from humility
clusters.desc: True leadership and influence arise from inner virtue, humility, and non-assertion rather than force.

clusters.label: Guard your own mind
clusters.desc: Protect your thoughts and focus on controlling your actions rather than trying to control external events or others.

clusters.label: Divine judgment awaits wrongdoing
clusters.desc: Those who commit wrongs or reject divine guidance will inevitably face judgment and punishment.

clusters.label: Stewardship requires justice
clusters.desc: Those entrusted with responsibility must manage it fairly and extend mercy to others as they have received it.

clusters.label: Virtue brings lasting reward
clusters.desc: Righteousness, hard work, and virtue consistently produce protection, respect, and lasting benefit, while wickedness leads to ruin.

clusters.label: Death makes all equal
clusters.desc: Everyone eventually dies and is forgotten regardless of achievement or status, making posthumous fame meaningless.

clusters.label: Virtue brings true happiness
clusters.desc: Happiness arises from living righteously, accepting what is natural, and acting justly with good intentions.

clusters.label: Truth transcends material reality
clusters.desc: Spiritual wisdom requires moving beyond ordinary knowledge and material attachments to perceive eternal transcendent reality.

clusters.label: Fulfill duties with reason
clusters.desc: Perform your responsibilities with clear thinking and virtue while maintaining awareness and not being overwhelmed by circumstances.

clusters.label: Character shows through action
clusters.desc: True character demonstrates itself through virtuous conduct aligned with principle, not merely by avoiding wrongdoing.

clusters.label: Work without attached reward
clusters.desc: Spiritual freedom comes from performing your duties diligently without attachment to personal benefits or outcomes.

clusters.label: Lead through moral example
clusters.desc: Effective leaders gain loyalty and sustain their communities through practicing virtue and setting a good example.

clusters.label: Wisdom succeeds, foolishness fails
clusters.desc: Wise discernment and humility lead to success and favor, while foolishness, arrogance, and emotional reactivity invite failure and ruin.

clusters.label: All existence ends in death
clusters.desc: All material beings and possessions eventually decay and die, rendering worldly pursuits and attachments ultimately meaningless.

clusters.label: Trust God's guidance and law
clusters.desc: Divine guidance is trustworthy, and following it requires personal accountability and forgiveness for spiritual integrity.

clusters.label: Greed blinds and destroys
clusters.desc: Unchecked greed for wealth blinds people to their mortality and inevitably leads to ruin and decay.

clusters.label: Wisdom exceeds all material wealth
clusters.desc: Wisdom, integrity, and understanding truth surpass any material possessions, status, or external wealth.

clusters.label: Authentic virtue hides its nature
clusters.desc: True virtue may involve withdrawing from power or appearing inactive, and the deepest virtue goes unrecognized because people misunderstand its true motivations.

clusters.label: Consciousness grounds all experience
clusters.desc: Reality unfolds through layers from physical sensations and matter through mental faculties to the Self as the unified conscious ground of all being.

clusters.label: Character reveals itself through action
clusters.desc: Your character is revealed by your actions, not your words or intentions, and small influences can transform the whole.

clusters.label: Rejecting truth brings certain destruction
clusters.desc: Those who reject divine signs, warnings, and guidance inevitably face destruction in both this world and the afterlife.

clusters.label: God is all-powerful and all-knowing
clusters.desc: God possesses absolute power and knowledge, created all things, and deserves exclusive worship as the only being worthy of devotion.

clusters.label: Live by reason and duty
clusters.desc: Fulfill your natural duty to act virtuously according to reason and serve the common good regardless of others' disapproval or your perceived insignificance.

clusters.label: Spiritual readiness transcends material goods
clusters.desc: Death is inevitable and only sincere spiritual preparation matters; material accumulation cannot protect against mortality.

clusters.label: Arrogance and ingratitude destroy
clusters.desc: Arrogant rejection of truth, ingratitude for abundance, and misapplied moral rules rooted in delusion inevitably cause self-destruction.

clusters.label: Virtue and integrity build character
clusters.desc: Genuine virtue, integrity, and right conduct rooted in sincere commitment earn respect and lasting satisfaction.

clusters.label: Detachment from desire liberates
clusters.desc: Freedom from all craving, attachment, and desire eliminates grief and fear, leading to enlightenment and peace.

clusters.label: Rational universal order
clusters.desc: The universe operates as one rational, harmonious whole where all events serve a unified cosmic purpose.

clusters.label: Reason governs practice
clusters.desc: Reason must govern over bodily concerns and daily distractions to maintain philosophical and moral integrity.

clusters.label: Leadership through integrity
clusters.desc: Effective leaders maintain harmony through personal virtue and restraint rather than force, edicts, or ostentatious display.

clusters.label: Honoring parents
clusters.desc: Treating parents with consistent respect, reverence, and proper ceremony is a fundamental human duty.

clusters.label: Knowledge liberates from rebirth
clusters.desc: Direct knowledge of consciousness as distinct from material nature brings liberation from suffering and death.

clusters.label: Virtue surpasses indulgence
clusters.desc: True pleasure comes from virtue and freedom from disturbance, not from pursuing sensual excess.

clusters.label: Judgment controls suffering
clusters.desc: Suffering stems from your judgment about events, not the events themselves; control your mind for peace.

clusters.label: Faith requires action
clusters.desc: Sincere faith in God combined with righteous action and generosity brings genuine security and divine reward.

clusters.label: Discipline brings inner peace
clusters.desc: Mastery over emotions and desires through meditation and controlled living leads to lasting equanimity.

clusters.label: Indulgence breeds suffering
clusters.desc: Lack of self-restraint and moral discipline traps people in endless cycles of harm and degradation.

clusters.label: Eternal consciousness pervades all
clusters.desc: An eternal, pure consciousness underlies all existence as the unchanging, transcendent ground of being.

clusters.label: Protect your integrity
clusters.desc: Maintain your principles by disregarding others' judgment, values, and actions except where duty requires.

clusters.label: Female power and character
clusters.desc: Women wield significant influence through either virtuous industry and wisdom or through seduction and cunning.

clusters.label: Self-correction before judgment
clusters.desc: Correct your own character and live by your principles before judging or criticizing others.

clusters.label: Spiritual purpose first
clusters.desc: Spiritual fulfillment and service to the divine should take priority over material possessions and self-reliance.

clusters.label: Divine reward for righteousness
clusters.desc: The righteous receive eternal paradise as divine reward while the wicked face eternal punishment.

clusters.label: Wisdom brings fulfillment
clusters.desc: Pursuing wisdom leads to life, good judgment, health, and lasting fulfillment.

clusters.label: Spiritual practice enables transcendence
clusters.desc: Dedicated spiritual practice leads to liberation from suffering and transcendence of death.

clusters.label: Desire prevents enlightenment
clusters.desc: Uncontrolled desire and worldly preoccupation are the primary obstacles to spiritual awakening and wisdom.

clusters.label: Ultimate reality pervades everything
clusters.desc: The ultimate reality simultaneously transcends and pervades all existence while sustaining all creation.

clusters.label: Accept death without fear
clusters.desc: Death should be accepted naturally and calmly as an inevitable part of life without resistance.

clusters.label: Faith and righteousness bring vindication
clusters.desc: Sincere faith and righteous action lead to divine reward and vindication despite hardship or opposition.

clusters.label: Respond to harm with kindness
clusters.desc: When wronged, respond with compassion and gentle teaching rather than retaliation or judgment.

clusters.label: All things are constantly changing
clusters.desc: Everything is subject to continuous change and impermanence, making attachment to particular things pointless.

clusters.label: Virtue surpasses circumstance
clusters.desc: Virtuous people naturally excel and elevate others regardless of circumstance, while petty people decline.

clusters.label: People resist understanding
clusters.desc: Many people refuse or fail to understand truth due to hardened hearts, familiarity, or lack of genuine commitment.

clusters.label: Inner virtue stays undisturbed
clusters.desc: True virtue lies in maintaining justice and purity of mind regardless of external circumstances or suffering.

clusters.label: Learning transforms character
clusters.desc: True learning requires an enlightened teacher and a receptive student willing to practice and transform through lived virtue.

clusters.label: Virtue matters more than appearance
clusters.desc: Genuine virtue is the only thing truly worthy of respect; external possessions and appearance have no real value.

clusters.label: God will resurrect and judge
clusters.desc: God has the power to resurrect the dead and will judge all people on the day of resurrection.
